perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice, provided however, that Paragraphs 3, 4, 5, and 7 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon fifteen (15) days written notice, provided however, that Paragraphs 3, 4, 5, and 7 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the written notice period required to terminate from thirty days to fifteen days. This creates a contradiction in the enforcement, considering which notice period would be enforced in the event of termination, with the rest of the contract expecting a longer period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of three (3) years unless terminated sooner as set forth below. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon fifteen (15) days written notice, provided however, that Paragraphs 3, 4, 5, and 7 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for termination from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days. The contradicted text, which can be in the same location, also states that the agreement may be terminated upon fifteen (15) days written notice. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original text stated thirty days and the contradicted text states fifteen days, meaning which notice period would be enforced is unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will disclose such information solely for the purpose of evaluating the possibility of entering into a business relationship between the parties hereto (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will disclose such information solely for the purpose of evaluating the possibility of entering into a business relationship or a merger between the parties hereto (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.explanation: The change adds "or a merger" to the defined "Purpose" of the agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement originally was intended to be for the evaluation of entering into a business relationship. Other sections of the contract, may not cover the case where a merger is pursuited, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms at least as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement. However, Recipient may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with a judicial or other governmental order provided that Recipient shall give Discloser written notice prior to such disclosure. Subject to Recipient’s obligations of non-disclosure, Recipient shall be free to use the residuals resulting from the use or access to the Confidential Information of Discloser. The term “residuals” means information in intangible form which is retained in the unaided memory of persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, without reference to such information in tangible or fixed form. Solely with respect to non-technical Confidential Information, Recipient’s obligations under this Section 3 shall expire one (1) year from the date of disclosure of such non-technical Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the "Purpose" as evaluating a 'business relationship'. The changed text expands the "Purpose" to include 'or a merger'. The contradicted text states that the 'Recipient shall use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose'. By broadening the definition of 'Purpose' to include a merger, the Recipient is now allowed to use confidential information for evaluating a merger. However, the original agreement may not have included terms or conditions relevant to a merger. Therefore, the change introduces a conflict because the recipient's use of information related to a merger may not be adequately governed by other terms in the original agreement, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information created by recipient (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction in the ownership of the confidential information. By stating that Confidential Information created by the recipient remains the property of the recipient, it directly contradicts the original statement that all Confidential Information remains the property of the discloser. This introduces uncertainty about who owns derivative works or analyses created by the recipient using the discloser's information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information created by recipient (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of recipient. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals. Upon the request of Discloser, Recipient shall, at Recipient’s option, either (a) return such materials to Discloser, or (b) certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated all confidential information remains the property of the discloser. The changed text, however, states that confidential information created by the recipient remains the property of the recipient. This directly contradicts the original text, as explained in the perturbation explanation, which in turn is further emphasized by the contradicted text that also states confidential information created by recipient remains the property of the recipient, creating a clear inconsistency about who owns derivative works or analyses created by the recipient.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may not be changed, modified, amended or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may be changed, modified, amended or supplemented by either a written or oral instrument acknowledged by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows for oral modifications to the contract, directly contradicting the original clause which requires all changes to be in writing and signed by both parties. This contradiction can lead to disputes over whether an oral agreement is legally binding and enforceable. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may be changed, modified, amended or supplemented by either a written or oral instrument acknowledged by both parties. If any provision of this Agreement is found by a proper authority to be unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect. Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that no remedy at law will afford Discloser adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, breach of Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction. P ACKETEER , I NC . O THER P ARTY : By: /s/ Dave Côté /s/ Brian NeSmith By: Dave Côté By: Brian NeSmith Title: President & CEO Title: President & CEO Date: Oct ober 20, 2007 Date: Oct ober 28, 2007
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified by a written instrument signed by both parties. The changed text modifies this to allow for oral modifications as well, provided they are acknowledged by both parties. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text regarding modification, explicitly states that the agreement 'may be changed, modified, amended or supplemented by either a written or oral instrument acknowledged by both parties.' This creates a direct contradiction, as the original text mandates written modifications, while the changed text and contradicted text allows for oral modifications, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty about which modifications are legally binding. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract (originally, and now the changed text along with the 'contradicted text') provide conflicting obligations regarding the form of contract modifications.
